name: Robyn (Rust-Powered Python Web Framework)

performance.notes: Rust runtime via PyO3; benchmarks show 2-4x faster than FastAPI

agent_readiness.notes: Python web framework with Rust runtime via PyO3. from robyn import Robyn. app = Robyn(__file__). @app.get('/hello') async def hello(request): return 'Hello'. WebSockets supported. Multiprocessing by default. Sub-processes for true parallelism (no GIL limitation). @app.startup_handler, @app.shutdown_handler. Middleware support. Template rendering (Jinja2). OpenAPI/Swagger generation in progress. Fast but ecosystem smaller than FastAPI. CLI: robyn --create for project scaffolding.

gotchas: Ecosystem much smaller than FastAPI (fewer extensions)
Pre-1.0 API stability — breaking changes possible
best_when: High-performance Python web services where FastAPI latency is a bottleneck
Projects needing true parallelism beyond GIL limitations
Greenfield projects willing to trade ecosystem for performance
avoid_when: Production systems requiring mature ecosystem (FastAPI/Starlette better)
Projects needing rich FastAPI extension ecosystem
summary: Robyn is a Python web framework backed by Rust runtime (PyO3) for high performance. 2-4x faster than FastAPI in benchmarks. from robyn import Robyn; @app.get('/') async def handler(request). True multiprocessing bypassing GIL. WebSocket support. Pre-1.0 but actively developed. Trade ecosystem maturity for raw performance — use FastAPI/Litestar for production unless performance is critical bottleneck.
